entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop produced by smallholder farmers in the study

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the rural irrigation scheme and study were located

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region where agricultural training is used to increase productivity and alleviate poverty

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL TRAINING
entities.description: A method to diffuse new technologies to increase productivity and alleviate rural poverty

entities.name: FARMER-TO-FARMER EXTENSION
entities.description: A cost-effective training approach where key farmers train intermediate farmers, who then influence ordinary farmers

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process by which farmers incorporate new agricultural technologies into their farming practices

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of production, specifically in the context of rice farming

entities.name: RURAL POVERTY
entities.description: The socio-economic condition that agricultural training aims to alleviate

entities.name: PADDY YIELD
entities.description: The amount of rice produced per hectare, used as a measure of productivity

entities.name: DIFFERENCE-IN-DIFFERENCES MODELS
entities.description: Statistical models used to assess the evolution of performance gaps as technologies spill over

entities.name: SPATIAL ECONOMETRIC MODELS
entities.description: Models used to examine the influence of social and geographical networks on technology adoption

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY SPILLOVER
entities.description: The process by which technologies spread from trained farmers to non-trained farmers

entities.name: SOCIAL AND GEOGRAPHICAL NETWORK
entities.description: The relationships and proximity between farmers that facilitate the dissemination of technology
